title: Tanker freight behaviour diverges after the conflict shock
section_path: Tanker freight behaviour diverges after the conflict shock
section_path_text: Tanker freight behaviour diverges after the conflict shock
summary:
Main
Conflict risk lifted tanker freight sharply, but crude and clean markets have responded differently as Hormuz exposure reshaped pricing. By Wanying Zhang
The conflict has triggered a sharp repricing across tanker freight, but the response has not been uniform. Crude and clean tankers both rallied as disruption risk centred on the Middle East Gulf forced charterers and owners to reassess exposure to the Strait of Hormuz.

children.title: Main
children.summary:
Conflict risk lifted tanker freight sharply, but crude and clean markets have responded differently as Hormuz exposure reshaped pricing. By Wanying Zhang
The conflict has triggered a sharp repricing across tanker freight, but the response has not been uniform.

children.title: Crude rally fragments as Atlantic tonnage rebuilds
section_path: Crude rally fragments as Atlantic tonnage rebuilds
children.section_path_text: Crude rally fragments as Atlantic tonnage rebuilds
children.summary:
Image reference: Στιγμιότυπο οθόνης 2026-04-27 103035.png | assets/2026-04-27_tanker-freight-behaviour-diverges-after-the-conflict-shock_img_cea3cf84ceb9ceb3cebcceb9cf8ccf84cf85_6a918d4d8aa8.png
Crude tanker freight reacted first and most aggressively. The VLCC market was the natural focal point, given its central role in lifting mainstream crude exports from the Middle East Gulf.

children.title: Clean freight stays resilient as longer haul employment tightens supply
section_path: Clean freight stays resilient as longer haul employment tightens supply
children.section_path_text: Clean freight stays resilient as longer haul employment tightens supply
children.summary:
Image reference: Στιγμιότυπο οθόνης 2026-04-27 103046.png | assets/2026-04-27_tanker-freight-behaviour-diverges-after-the-conflict-shock_img_cea3cf84ceb9ceb3cebcceb9cf8ccf84cf85_8a004ce70bc0.png
Clean freight has followed a different trajectory. Rates have also moved sharply higher across vessel classes, but unlike crude freight, the clean market has shown far less correction.

children.title: Reopening Hormuz may ease risk, but not reset freight
section_path: Reopening Hormuz may ease risk, but not reset freight
children.section_path_text: Reopening Hormuz may ease risk, but not reset freight
children.summary: For crude tankers, particularly VLCCs, a recovery in Strait of Hormuz transits would bring cargoes back into a market where the tonnage is available, but not necessarily easy to secure. Total non sanctioned VLCC ballast is around 26% above the pre conflict average , suggesting there is enough vessel supply in the system to absorb a return of Middle East Gulf crude volumes.
